text:
GUARDANT HEALTH / MOLECULAR PATHOLOGY LABORATORY
LIQUID BIOPSY REPORT — CELL-FREE DNA (cfDNA) COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING
Guardant360 CDx Assay

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SPECIMEN AND ORDERING INFORMATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Patient Name:          Yuen, Margaret
Date of Birth:         [ON FILE]
Medical Record Number: [ON FILE]
Ordering Physician:    [Treating Oncologist], MD
Institution:           [Academic Medical Center / Thoracic Oncology Program]
Specimen Type:         Peripheral blood (plasma, cell-free DNA)
Specimen Collection:   Peripheral venipuncture — 2 x 10 mL Streck Cell-Free DNA BCT tubes
Date Specimen Received: [DATE]
Date of Report:        [DATE]
Assay:                 Guardant360 CDx (FDA-cleared companion diagnostic)
Sample Adequacy:       ADEQUATE — sufficient cfDNA yield for comprehensive analysis

Clinical Indication: Non-small cell lung carcinoma, adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant, Stage IIIB, initially diagnosed, treated with upfront osi (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily. Now with clinical and radiographic evidence of disease progression at approximately 14 months (systemic and intracranial). Liquid biopsy ordered to assess for acquired resistance mechanisms, specifically EGFR T790M mutation. Prior tissue NGS (500-gene solid tumor panel, tumor-only) demonstrated EGFR L858R (VAF 42%), TP53 R248W, no prior T790M detected.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
GROSS DESCRIPTION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Received: Two (2) Streck Cell-Free DNA Blood Collection Tubes (10 mL each), labeled with patient name and medical record number, containing peripheral whole blood collected for plasma cfDNA extraction. Tubes were received at ambient temperature within acceptable cold-chain parameters (collected and shipped per Guardant Health specimen collection protocols). Upon receipt, tubes were centrifuged at 1,600 x g for 10 minutes, followed by plasma separation and a second centrifugation at 16,000 x g for 10 minutes to remove residual cellular material. Plasma volume obtained: 3.8 mL (combined). Cell-free DNA was extracted using validated column-based methodology. Library preparation performed using Guardant360 proprietary digital sequencing technology (digital error suppression). Total cfDNA input: 14.2 ng (adequate). Mean depth of coverage: >15,000x (median unique depth). The sample met all internal quality control thresholds for sequencing coverage, error rate, and background noise estimation. Germline variants cannot be fully distinguished from somatic variants in this tumor-only cfDNA assay; results are reported in the context of known somatic alterations and population allele frequencies.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS — SOMATIC VARIANTS DETECTED
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

I. CLINICALLY ACTIONABLE / TIER I VARIANTS

1. EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G), Exon 21
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 3.8%
   Classification: Pathogenic — Sensitizing EGFR mutation (Class I, activating)
   Interpretation: This is the patient's known primary driver mutation, first detected in tissue at diagnosis (VAF 42% in FFPE core biopsy). Detection in plasma ctDNA at VAF 3.8% is consistent with residual circulating tumor DNA shedding from progressing disease. The relatively low VAF reflects the partial suppression of dominant EGFR L858R clone during Tagrisso treatment, with re-emergence corresponding to clinical progression. This mutation confirms tumor-derived cfDNA is present and analyzable in this specimen.

2. EGFR p.T790M (c.2369C>T), Exon 20
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 1.6%
   Classification: Pathogenic — Acquired resistance mutation
   Interpretation: EGFR T790M is detected at low VAF (1.6%) in this plasma specimen. In the context of 1st- and 2nd-generation EGFR TKI treatment (erlo, Iressa, afat, Vizimpro), T790M is the most common acquired resistance mechanism (~50-65% of cases), and in that setting would represent a targetable resistance mutation amenable to Tagrisso therapy. However, this patient was treated with upfront osi (3rd-generation TKI), in which T790M does NOT constitute a primary resistance mechanism. Detection of T790M in the setting of upfront osi is ATYPICAL and may reflect: (a) a pre-existing low-level subclonal T790M population present at baseline that was below the limit of detection of the original diagnostic PCR/NGS, now enriched under selective therapeutic pressure; (b) a coincidental co-occurring resistance biology; or (c) an emerging secondary EGFR pathway alteration. The clinical significance in the context of third-generation Tagrisso treatment requires further investigation. Notably, EGFR C797S — the canonical resistance mutation to osi — is NOT detected (see below).

II. ADDITIONAL SOMATIC VARIANTS DETECTED

3. TP53 p.R248W (c.742C>T), Exon 7
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 5.2%
   Classification: Pathogenic — Known hotspot loss-of-function mutation in TP53 tumor suppressor
   Interpretation: TP53 R248W was previously identified in diagnostic tissue NGS (VAF 38%) and is confirmed in the current liquid biopsy (VAF 5.2%). The lower VAF in plasma relative to tissue is expected and reflects the dilution of ctDNA in total cfDNA. TP53 co-mutations in EGFR-mutant NSCLC have been associated with inferior outcomes on EGFR TKI therapy and potentially with accelerated resistance evolution. This variant serves as an additional ctDNA shedding marker confirming specimen adequacy and tumor-derived signal.

4. ARID1A frameshift mutation (specific amino acid change pending confirmation — see below)
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 0.9%
   Classification: Likely pathogenic (subclonal, low-VAF)
   Interpretation: A low-VAF ARID1A frameshift variant was previously identified in diagnostic tissue NGS (ARID1A p.E1814*, VAF 18%) as a variant of uncertain significance. The current cfDNA assay detects a low-level ARID1A frameshift (VAF 0.9%), which is consistent with ctDNA representation of this previously identified subclonal variant. ARID1A loss-of-function is associated with SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex dysfunction; its role in resistance to EGFR TKI therapy remains under investigation. This variant is subclonal and its specific sequence-level correlation with the prior tissue variant is pending confirmatory analysis.

III. VARIANTS NOT DETECTED (CLINICALLY RELEVANT NEGATIVE FINDINGS)

- EGFR exon 20 p.C797S: NOT DETECTED
  Interpretation: Absence of C797S is a critical negative finding. C797S is the most common mechanism of resistance to Tagrisso (3rd-generation EGFR TKI) and, if present in trans with T790M, would indicate triple-mutant (L858R/T790M/C797S) resistance that lacks a currently approved targeted option. The absence of C797S combined with the presence of T790M at low VAF does not support a canonical C797S-mediated Tagrisso resistance mechanism. Further evaluation for bypass resistance tracks (e.g., MET amplification, HER2 amplification, histologic transformation) is warranted and tissue re-biopsy may be considered.

- EGFR exon 20 insertions: NOT DETECTED
- EGFR exon 19 deletion: NOT DETECTED
- MET amplification: NOT DETECTED (note: copy number assessment in cfDNA assays has lower sensitivity relative to tissue-based NGS; a negative result does not exclude low-level MET amplification)
- ERBB2 (HER2) amplification: NOT DETECTED
- BRAF p.V600E: NOT DETECTED
- KRAS mutations: NOT DETECTED
- ALK fusion: NOT DETECTED
- ROS1 fusion: NOT DETECTED
- RET fusion: NOT DETECTED
- NTRK1/2/3 fusions: NOT DETECTED
- PIK3CA hotspot mutations: NOT DETECTED
- FGFR1/2/3 amplification: NOT DETECTED

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
ANCILLARY STUDIES / ADDITIONAL ANALYTES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB) from ctDNA:   Low (estimated from plasma cfDNA; plasma-derived TMB has lower quantitative precision than tissue-based TMB; tissue re-biopsy recommended for definitive TMB assessment if immunotherapy eligibility is being considered)

Microsatellite Instability (MSI) from cfDNA: STABLE (MSI-H not detected; consistent with prior tissue result)

Specimen Ctrlomics / Clonal Hematopoiesis Flag:   Several variants at very low VAF were identified but were assessed as below the threshold for reportable somatic alterations. Clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential (CHIP) variants were assessed using Guardant's internal algorithm; no CHIP-associated mutations (DNMT3A, TET2, ASXL1, JAK2) were detected above the reporting threshold.

SEQUENCING QUALITY METRICS:
- Mean unique depth of coverage: 18,340x
- Number of reportable genomic loci assessed: 74 genes (Guardant360 panel)
- Sample quality score: PASS
- Background error rate: within expected range

NOTE — PENDING STUDY: Correlation of cfDNA ARID1A frameshift with specific tissue ARID1A p.E1814* previously identified will be performed upon receipt of additional sequence-level data; updated report to be issued if discordance is identified. [Status: PENDING — expected turnaround 3-5 business days from report date.]

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SUMMARY / MOLECULAR INTERPRETATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

SPECIMEN ADEQUACY: Adequate — circulating tumor DNA detected above established assay limits of detection.

KEY FINDINGS:
1. EGFR L858R confirmed in plasma ctDNA (VAF 3.8%) — consistent with active disease burden from the known primary EGFR driver mutation.
2. EGFR T790M DETECTED at low VAF (1.6%) — acquired resistance mutation detected. In the context of upfront (first-line) Tagrisso treatment, T790M positivity is atypical and does not conform to the expected osi resistance profile. This finding is most consistent with a low-level pre-existing subclonal T790M population that was not detectable at the time of original diagnosis (below the sensitivity threshold of initial cobas PCR/NGS testing) and has been enriched under selective pressure of osi therapy. Alternative possibility of a true de novo second-hit T790M emerging as an osi resistance mechanism cannot be fully excluded but is considered biologically less likely.
3. EGFR C797S NOT detected — excludes the most common canonical resistance mechanism to osi, suggesting additional or alternative bypass resistance pathways may be operative.
4. No MET amplification detected by cfDNA assay — however, sensitivity of plasma cfDNA-based copy number assessment is limited; tissue re-biopsy with comprehensive genomic profiling should be considered to fully exclude bypass track mechanisms (MET amplification, ERBB2 amplification, oncogenic fusions) that are not optimally assessed by liquid biopsy.
5. TP53 R248W confirmed as co-mutation.

CLINICAL INTERPRETATION:
The detection of EGFR T790M in the context of upfront Tagrisso failure is uncommon. The treating oncologist should consider:
(a) The possibility that this T790M is not the primary driver of resistance, and that other bypass mechanisms not captured at adequate sensitivity by this cfDNA assay (e.g., MET amplification, histologic transformation to small cell) may be operationally responsible for clinical progression.
(b) Tissue re-biopsy of the accessible progressing lesion (right upper lobe mass) for comprehensive genomic profiling and histologic assessment (to exclude small cell or squamous transformation).
(c) Given the presence of T790M, novel combination approaches targeting EGFR L858R/T790M (e.g., EGFR-MET bispecific antibodies, amivantamab-lazertinib) and/or platinum-based chemotherapy may be considered in the context of clinical trial enrollment or standard of care, per multidisciplinary team decision.

This report is issued as a laboratory finding only. Therapeutic decisions remain the responsibility of the treating physician in consultation with the clinical team.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DIAGNOSIS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

PERIPHERAL BLOOD, CELL-FREE DNA, GUARDANT360 CDx LIQUID BIOPSY:
- EGFR p.L858R DETECTED (VAF 3.8%) — known primary driver mutation, confirmed
- EGFR p.T790M DETECTED (VAF 1.6%) — acquired resistance mutation, likely subclonal enrichment under osi; see interpretation above
- EGFR p.C797S: NOT DETECTED
- MET amplification: NOT DETECTED (note caveat regarding cfDNA copy number sensitivity)
- TP53 p.R248W DETECTED (VAF 5.2%) — co-mutation, consistent with prior tissue result
- ARID1A frameshift DETECTED (VAF 0.9%, low-level subclonal) — confirmation of sequence concordance with prior tissue variant pending

Signed electronically by:
[Molecular Pathologist, MD, PhD]
Board Certified, Molecular Genetic Pathology and Anatomic Pathology
Director, Molecular Diagnostics Laboratory
[Institution]

Case reviewed and co-signed:
[Senior Molecular Pathologist, MD]
Molecular Genetic Pathology

Report Status: FINAL (amendment pending for ARID1A sequence-level correlation)
